claim: Running multiple agents in parallel without communication is an anti-pattern that wastes tokens inefficiently compared to fewer agents that communicate
actionable: Design agent systems with careful coordination and fewer parallel agents rather than spawning many independent agents
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Critique loops with high-quality models where one agent generates and another critiques produce much higher code quality than a single agent, despite higher latency
actionable: Implement multi-agent critique loops using high-quality models (e.g., Opus 4.6) to review code from generation agents, accepting longer latencies for better quality
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Token budgets alone are misleading metrics; the real bottleneck in AI-augmented development is PR review and deployment stability, not token consumption
actionable: When scaling AI-generated code, invest heavily in PR review automation and CI/CD testing rather than just monitoring token spend
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Good models write code with fewer bugs on average than average humans, but since they produce vastly more code, more total bugs reach production unless you have rigorous PR review
actionable: Establish strict PR review gates (both manual and automated) using high-quality models to catch the increased bug volume from AI code generation
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Tangle enables efficient, reproducible ML workflows with content-based caching that automatically deduplicates work across teams without explicit coordination
actionable: Use Tangle for collaborative ML experiments to get automatic network effects from deduplication when other teams run similar preprocessing steps
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Tangent auto-research loops that iteratively modify and test code improvements have succeeded even on already-optimized systems, finding improvements where manual optimization had reached plateaus
actionable: Apply auto-research loops to any problem with measurable metrics, even if you believe the system is already well-optimized; the loop may find gains humans missed
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Auto-research is good at obvious optimizations that humans didn't do, but weak at non-obvious discoveries requiring multiple days of human thinking
actionable: Use auto-research for systematic optimization of known metrics, but maintain human involvement for novel, non-obvious problem domains
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: SimGym uses decades of historical customer behavior data denoised with collaborative filtering to train agents that predict real-world outcomes (0.7 correlation with add-to-cart events) without A/B tests
actionable: For simulation-based optimization, aggregate noisy historical data across many non-isolated experiments and use collaborative filtering to extract signal for training predictive agents
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Liquid neural networks (non-transformer architecture) provide better efficiency than transformers for long-context, low-latency, or small-model scenarios
actionable: When optimizing for latency or model size with long context, evaluate Liquid-based models as alternatives to transformers for distillation targets
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Liquid models are subquadratic in context length (not quadratic like transformers), making them suitable for very long-context applications where transformers become prohibitively expensive
actionable: For applications requiring >100k token context or strict latency budgets under 30ms, prioritize Liquid architecture over transformers
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: December 2025 marked a model quality inflection point where Shopify saw simultaneous adoption across CLI tools and IDE-based tools accelerate dramatically
actionable: Monitor model capability inflections by tracking tool adoption curves; these often signal regime shifts in what's possible with AI
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Stack-based PR workflows (Graphite) may be necessary to handle the volume of AI-generated PRs; traditional git-based PR review is becoming a bottleneck
actionable: For high-velocity AI-driven development, evaluate stack-based workflow tools (Graphite, Sapling) instead of traditional GitHub flow
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Sydney's personality emergence at Bing was not accidental; intentional personality shaping using techniques from Alice (Yandex) was critical to user engagement
actionable: When designing conversational AI, deliberately shape personality toward polite but slightly edgy tone; don't rely on emergent personality alone
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Counterfactual modeling at merchant scale can predict which interventions (discounts, layout changes) would improve outcomes without running A/B tests
actionable: Build counterfactual models of customer journeys to identify intervention opportunities; use causal inference to score which changes will improve metrics
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin

claim: Chinese Restaurant Process (CRP) clustering enables discovering emergent behavior patterns across customers without predefined categories
actionable: For unsupervised discovery of customer segments or behavior clusters, apply Chinese Restaurant Process instead of k-means or other fixed-k methods
source_title: AI-Native Engineering: 100% adoption, 5x search throughput, unlimited tokens — Mikhail Parakhin
